Ray Dalio.name: Bridgewater Associates Investment Committee
Ray Dalio.description: The strategic brain of the world's largest hedge fund, shaping macro capital flows and influencing sovereign wealth fund allocations globally.

Ray Dalio.name: China-US Exchange Foundation (CUSEF) Advisory Network
Ray Dalio.description: A Track II diplomacy platform where Dalio has engaged as an informal bridge between US financial elites and Chinese state leadership.

Ray Dalio.name: Council on Foreign Relations
Ray Dalio.description: The preeminent US foreign policy establishment network connecting finance, government, and think-tank power into a coherent elite consensus-building body.

Karol Nawrocki.name: Polish Presidential Office
Karol Nawrocki.description: The executive institutional seat of Polish state power, positioned at the front line of NATO's eastern flank and EU-Russia boundary politics.

Karol Nawrocki.name: Law and Justice (PiS) Aligned Institutional Network
Karol Nawrocki.description: The informal coalition of PiS-affiliated state institutions, judiciary appointees, and cultural bodies that Nawrocki emerged from and continues to anchor.

Karol Nawrocki.name: Institute of National Remembrance (IPN) Leadership Alumni
Karol Nawrocki.description: A network of Polish historical-memory and national-identity institutions that shapes Poland's geopolitical narrative toward Russia, Germany, and the EU.

Pan Gongsheng.name: People's Bank of China Monetary Policy Committee
Pan Gongsheng.description: China's central monetary authority that sets interest rates, manages the yuan, and steers the world's second-largest economy's financial stability.

Pan Gongsheng.name: Financial Stability and Development Committee (FSDC)
Pan Gongsheng.description: China's top-level inter-agency financial regulatory coordination body, chaired by the State Council, where the PBoC Governor holds decisive operational influence.

Pan Gongsheng.name: Bank for International Settlements (BIS) Board of Directors
Pan Gongsheng.description: The central bank of central banks, where Pan represents China's growing role in reshaping global monetary governance and reserve currency frameworks.

Bidzina Ivanishvili & family.name: Georgian Dream Party Leadership Council
Bidzina Ivanishvili & family.description: The ruling political vehicle Ivanishvili founded and continues to control, determining Georgia's strategic orientation between Russia, the EU, and the US.

Bidzina Ivanishvili & family.name: Ivanishvili Family Office & Cartu Group
Bidzina Ivanishvili & family.description: The private financial and business empire through which Ivanishvili controls significant Georgian banking, real estate, and media assets, underpinning his political dominance.

Bidzina Ivanishvili & family.name: Eurasian Economic Orbit Informal Financier Network
Bidzina Ivanishvili & family.description: A loose but trackable cluster of post-Soviet oligarchs with Russian-linked capital whose strategic hedging shapes small-state alignment decisions across the former Soviet periphery.

Gianluigi Aponte.name: MSC (Mediterranean Shipping Company) Executive Group
Gianluigi Aponte.description: The world's largest container shipping company by fleet capacity, whose routing and pricing decisions structurally shape global trade logistics and supply chain geopolitics.

Gianluigi Aponte.name: Aponte Family Holding (TIL — Terminal Investment Limited)
Gianluigi Aponte.description: MSC's port terminal acquisition arm, which controls strategic port infrastructure across Europe, Africa, and Asia, giving the Aponte family leverage over critical maritime chokepoints.

Gianluigi Aponte.name: World Shipping Council
Gianluigi Aponte.description: The principal global industry body representing container carriers, through which Aponte-aligned interests influence international maritime regulation and trade policy.

John Malone.name: Liberty Media / Liberty TripAdvisor Holdings Board
John Malone.description: Malone's primary corporate holding constellation, controlling significant stakes in global media, sports, and communications assets that shape Western information infrastructure.

John Malone.name: Charter Communications Board
John Malone.description: One of the largest US broadband and cable operators, where Malone's structural influence shapes American domestic information access and media distribution.

John Malone.name: Discovery-Warner Bros. (WBD) Founding Shareholder Network
John Malone.description: The shareholder coalition that created Warner Bros. Discovery, giving Malone lasting influence over a major global English-language media and content power.

Alisher Usmanov.name: USM Holdings Executive Circle
Alisher Usmanov.description: Usmanov's core diversified holding company spanning metals, telecoms, and technology investments across Russia, Europe, and Asia, serving as his primary geopolitical financial instrument.

Alisher Usmanov.name: Metalloinvest Board
Alisher Usmanov.description: One of the world's largest iron ore and steel producers, whose output and trade relationships embed Usmanov in global commodity markets and Russian state-industrial networks.

Alisher Usmanov.name: Sanctioned Russian Elite Network (EU/UK/US Designation Cluster)
Alisher Usmanov.description: The formally designated cluster of Putin-linked oligarchs whose frozen assets, legal battles, and back-channel relationships remain a live instrument of Western-Russia financial warfare.

Paul Singer.name: Elliott Investment Management Executive Committee
Paul Singer.description: One of the world's most aggressive activist and distressed-debt hedge funds, with a track record of forcing sovereign debt restructurings and corporate governance changes globally.

Paul Singer.name: Republican Jewish Coalition (RJC) Leadership Donor Circle
Paul Singer.description: A major US political donor network shaping Republican Party foreign policy positions, particularly regarding Israel, Iran, and US-Middle East strategy.

Paul Singer.name: Manhattan Institute Board of Trustees
Paul Singer.description: A leading US center-right think tank that produces policy frameworks influencing financial regulation, urban governance, and national security doctrine.

Yuri Kovalchuk.name: Bank Rossiya Shareholder & Board
Yuri Kovalchuk.description: The Kremlin-linked private bank designated by the US as Putin's personal cashbox, serving as a key node for financing inner-circle projects and circumventing Western sanctions.

Yuri Kovalchuk.name: National Media Group (NMG) Controlling Ownership
Yuri Kovalchuk.description: Kovalchuk's media holding that controls major Russian TV channels, making him the dominant architect of pro-Kremlin domestic information and propaganda infrastructure.

Yuri Kovalchuk.name: Putin Siloviki-Financial Inner Circle
Yuri Kovalchuk.description: The tight, named network of St. Petersburg-origin loyalists — including Timchenko, Rotenberg, and Kovalchuk — who collectively manage off-balance-sheet Kremlin financial and political power.

Ronald Lauder.name: World Jewish Congress (WJC) Presidential Office
Ronald Lauder.description: The global diplomatic representative body of Jewish communities worldwide, through which Lauder engages heads of state on antisemitism, Holocaust restitution, and Israel-diaspora relations.

Ronald Lauder.name: Estée Lauder Companies Board (Lauder Family Shareholders)
Ronald Lauder.description: The family shareholding bloc in one of the world's largest luxury cosmetics corporations, providing Lauder with global business access and soft-power reach across consumer markets.

Ronald Lauder.name: Central European University (CEU) & Open Society-Adjacent Donor Network
Ronald Lauder.description: The cluster of Central and Eastern European civil society, education, and restitution initiatives where Lauder's philanthropy intersects with broader democratic governance and Jewish heritage agendas.

C.C. Wei.name: TSMC Board of Directors
C.C. Wei.description: Governs the world's most critical semiconductor manufacturer, whose advanced chip production is the central chokepoint in US-China technology competition.

C.C. Wei.name: US-Taiwan Defense Industrial Cooperation Framework
C.C. Wei.description: Informal but operationally significant coordination structure linking TSMC's fab expansion decisions to US CHIPS Act policy and Pentagon supply-chain security priorities.

C.C. Wei.name: Semiconductor Industry Association (SIA)
C.C. Wei.description: The primary US-based industry lobbying and standards body that shapes export control policy, R&D investment, and allied chip-sector coordination globally.

Safra Catz.name: Oracle Executive Leadership Council
Safra Catz.description: Directs Oracle's global cloud and data infrastructure strategy, increasingly central to US government and military cloud contracts including the DoD's JEDI/JWCC ecosystem.

Safra Catz.name: Trump Presidential Transition Advisory Network (2016/2024)
Safra Catz.description: Informal but documented advisory cluster connecting Silicon Valley executives to Republican executive-branch policy formation, giving Oracle preferential positioning in federal technology procurement.

Safra Catz.name: Business Roundtable
Safra Catz.description: The premier US CEO lobbying consortium that coordinates corporate positions on trade, technology regulation, and national competitiveness policy with Congress and the executive branch.

Leonid Mikhelson & family.name: NOVATEK Board & Controlling Shareholder Group
Leonid Mikhelson & family.description: Controls Russia's largest independent natural gas producer and the Arctic LNG 2 project, making Mikhelson a pivotal node in Russia's strategy to redirect energy exports toward Asia under sanctions.

Leonid Mikhelson & family.name: Russia-China Arctic Energy Partnership (CNPC-NOVATEK Joint Ventures)
Leonid Mikhelson & family.description: A set of equity and offtake agreements between NOVATEK and Chinese state energy firms that structurally bind Russian Arctic LNG supply to Chinese demand, circumventing Western sanctions regimes.

Leonid Mikhelson & family.name: Russian Union of Industrialists and Entrepreneurs (RSPP)
Leonid Mikhelson & family.description: The principal organized voice of Russian big business that mediates between oligarchic capital and the Kremlin, providing Mikhelson a sanctioned channel of political access within Russia's managed economy.

Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.name: AKP Party Executive and Presidential Administration
Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.description: The ruling institutional apparatus through which Erdoğan exercises consolidated executive authority over Turkish foreign, economic, and security policy.

Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.name: NATO North Atlantic Council
Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.description: Turkey's membership in the alliance gives Erdoğan structural veto leverage over NATO enlargement and basing decisions, used as a bargaining chip between Washington and Moscow.

Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.name: Organization of Turkic States (OTS)
Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.description: A Ankara-led multilateral body connecting Turkey with Central Asian Turkic republics, serving as Erdoğan's primary vehicle for projecting pan-Turkic geopolitical influence as a counterweight to both Russian and Chinese regional dominance.

Shah Karim al-Husayni (Aga Khan IV).name: Aga Khan Development Network (AKDN)
Shah Karim al-Husayni (Aga Khan IV).description: A $1B+ per year transnational development conglomerate operating in over 30 countries that functions as a quasi-sovereign soft-power and infrastructure actor in fragile states across South Asia, Central Asia, and East Africa.

Shah Karim al-Husayni (Aga Khan IV).name: Ismaili Imamate Diplomatic Corps
Shah Karim al-Husayni (Aga Khan IV).description: A unique para-diplomatic institutional presence recognized by multiple states, enabling the Aga Khan to negotiate directly with heads of government and serve as a neutral interlocutor in geopolitically sensitive regions.

Shah Karim al-Husayni (Aga Khan IV).name: Global Centre for Pluralism (GCP)
Shah Karim al-Husayni (Aga Khan IV).description: A Ottawa-based institution co-founded with the Canadian government that positions the Imamate as a normative authority on multicultural governance, extending influence into Western liberal institutional networks.

Andrew Forrest & family.name: Fortescue Ltd Board
Andrew Forrest & family.description: Controls Australia's third-largest iron ore exporter and a major green hydrogen development platform, giving Forrest structural leverage in both China-dependent commodity markets and the global energy transition.

Andrew Forrest & family.name: Global Green Hydrogen Alliance (Fortescue Future Industries Network)
Andrew Forrest & family.description: An industry-led coalition anchored by Fortescue's FFI division that is negotiating large-scale green hydrogen supply agreements with governments across Europe, Asia, and the Middle East, shaping the geopolitics of post-carbon energy.

Andrew Forrest & family.name: Minderoo Foundation
Andrew Forrest & family.description: A major Australian philanthropic vehicle with global health, ocean, and anti-slavery programs that provides Forrest access to multilateral institutions, heads of state, and UN-system agenda-setting processes.

Melinda French Gates.name: Pivotal Ventures Investment and Incubation Company
Melinda French Gates.description: Her independent investment and incubation firm targeting gender equity and social progress, operating separately from the Gates Foundation and signaling an autonomous philanthropic-capital power base.

Melinda French Gates.name: Gates Foundation Alumni and Co-Funding Network
Melinda French Gates.description: An informal but dense cluster of co-grantees, former foundation executives, and aligned institutional donors whose global health and development agendas were shaped by French Gates's years as co-chair.

Melinda French Gates.name: UN Women Generation Equality Forum Partner Coalition
Melinda French Gates.description: A multilateral commitment platform linking major philanthropies, governments, and corporations around gender equality targets, in which French Gates is a leading financial and normative anchor.

Bill Gates.name: Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Board
Bill Gates.description: The world's largest private charitable foundation, which exercises outsized influence over global health policy, agricultural development, and pandemic preparedness through its funding relationships with WHO, GAVI, and national governments.

Bill Gates.name: Breakthrough Energy Coalition
Bill Gates.description: A Gates-founded consortium of billionaire investors and sovereign funds financing frontier clean-energy technology, directly shaping the industrial policy and R&D priorities of the US, EU, and allied governments in the energy transition.

Bill Gates.name: Global Health Security Agenda (GHSA) Private Sector Roundtable
Bill Gates.description: An informal cluster of major health-security philanthropies and industry actors that coordinates with G7 governments on pandemic preparedness financing and normative frameworks, where the Gates Foundation is the dominant non-state actor.

Patrick Collison.name: Stripe Executive Leadership and Board
Patrick Collison.description: Leads the world's most valuable private payments infrastructure company, whose cross-border transaction rails give Collison structural visibility into global capital flows and leverage over digital commerce regulation.

Patrick Collison.name: Arc Institute Board
Patrick Collison.description: A Collison-co-founded independent biomedical research organization designed to circumvent traditional NIH/academic funding constraints, representing a growing trend of tech-billionaire parallel scientific institutions that rival public research agencies.

Patrick Collison.name: Stripe Press / Progress Studies Network
Patrick Collison.description: An intellectual and publishing platform that has seeded the 'Progress Studies' movement, positioning Collison as a normative agenda-setter in debates about science funding, state capacity, and long-run economic growth within US and EU policy circles.

Aziz Akhannouch & family.name: Moroccan Council of Government (Prime Minister's Cabinet)
Aziz Akhannouch & family.description: As Morocco's Prime Minister, Akhannouch chairs the executive body that sets national economic, energy, and foreign investment policy, directly linking his business empire to state decision-making.

Aziz Akhannouch & family.name: Akwa Group Holding Company
Aziz Akhannouch & family.description: The family's dominant petroleum distribution and real estate conglomerate, whose market position across North and West Africa makes Akhannouch a key private-sector node in the region's energy and infrastructure landscape.

Aziz Akhannouch & family.name: Morocco-EU Advanced Status Partnership Council
Aziz Akhannouch & family.description: The bilateral institutional framework governing Morocco's privileged relationship with the European Union on trade, migration, and energy, in which Akhannouch's government is the primary Moroccan interlocutor shaping North Africa's geopolitical alignment.

Eric Yuan & family.name: Zoom Video Communications Board of Directors
Eric Yuan & family.description: The governing body of Zoom, a globally critical video communications platform whose infrastructure and data flows sit at the center of US-China tech trust disputes and export control debates.

Eric Yuan & family.name: US-China Business Council
Eric Yuan & family.description: A Washington-based lobby and dialogue forum representing American companies with China interests, shaping bilateral trade and technology policy at the executive and congressional level.

Sundar Pichai.name: Alphabet Executive Leadership Team
Sundar Pichai.description: The apex decision-making body of Alphabet/Google, controlling the world's dominant search, AI, cloud, and advertising infrastructure with direct leverage over information flows globally.

Sundar Pichai.name: US AI Safety Institute Consortium (AISIC)
Sundar Pichai.description: A NIST-convened industry body shaping US federal AI safety standards, positioning Google at the center of the regulatory framework that will govern global AI competition with China.

Sundar Pichai.name: OECD Global Partnership on AI (GPAI)
Sundar Pichai.description: A multilateral forum of governments and tech industry leaders setting international AI governance norms, where Google's participation influences rules affecting technology competition between democratic and authoritarian blocs.

Prabowo Subianto.name: ASEAN Defence Ministers' Meeting (ADMM)
Prabowo Subianto.description: The principal regional forum for Southeast Asian defence cooperation, where Indonesia under Prabowo is asserting strategic autonomy between US and Chinese military influence.

Prabowo Subianto.name: Indonesian Cabinet (Kabinet Merah Putih)
Prabowo Subianto.description: Prabowo's unified executive government controlling the world's fourth most populous nation, managing Indonesia's pivotal role in critical mineral supply chains and Indo-Pacific geopolitics.

Prabowo Subianto.name: G20 Sherpa Network
Prabowo Subianto.description: Indonesia's ongoing G20 membership apparatus gives Prabowo direct access to the forum coordinating global macroeconomic policy and development finance, amplifying Jakarta's voice in US-China economic rivalry.

Russell M. Nelson.name: First Presidency of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
Russell M. Nelson.description: The supreme governing council of a 17-million-member transnational religious institution with significant financial assets, global humanitarian operations, and political influence concentrated in the US Mountain West.

Russell M. Nelson.name: LDS Church Welfare and Self-Reliance Services
Russell M. Nelson.description: The Church's global humanitarian and development arm, operating in over 190 countries and providing soft-power reach and community infrastructure that intersects with US foreign policy goals in contested regions.

Donald Trump.name: US National Security Council (NSC)
Donald Trump.description: The principal interagency body coordinating American foreign policy, military strategy, and intelligence, through which Trump directs the full coercive and diplomatic power of the United States.

Donald Trump.name: Republican Party Leadership Council
Donald Trump.description: The dominant faction of the US Republican Party, which Trump controls as its de facto leader, shaping legislative agendas on tariffs, NATO commitments, and technology competition with China.

Donald Trump.name: G7 Heads of Government
Donald Trump.description: The forum of leaders of the world's major advanced democracies, where Trump's positions on trade, Ukraine, and China directly set or disrupt the coordinated Western strategic posture.

Abdelmadjid Tebboune.name: African Union Assembly of Heads of State
Abdelmadjid Tebboune.description: The AU's supreme organ, where Algeria leverages its size, gas wealth, and military capacity to project influence over Sahel security architecture and resist Western and Gulf interventionism across the continent.

Abdelmadjid Tebboune.name: Arab League Council
Abdelmadjid Tebboune.description: The principal pan-Arab intergovernmental body, in which Algeria plays a balancing role between Gulf monarchies, the Palestinian cause, and external powers competing for North African and Middle Eastern influence.

Abdelmadjid Tebboune.name: OPEC+ Ministerial Meeting (via Algerian Energy Ministry)
Abdelmadjid Tebboune.description: Algeria's membership in the OPEC+ production coordination framework gives Tebboune's government leverage over global energy prices and a platform for aligning with Russia and Gulf states on hydrocarbon strategy.

Alexander Lukashenko.name: Union State of Russia and Belarus Supreme State Council
Alexander Lukashenko.description: The governing body of the Russia-Belarus political and military union, through which Lukashenko has traded sovereignty for survival, allowing Russian nuclear and troop deployments on NATO's eastern flank.

Alexander Lukashenko.name: Collective Security Treaty Organization (CSTO) Heads of State Council
Alexander Lukashenko.description: Russia-led post-Soviet mutual defence alliance whose cohesion and credibility Lukashenko's dependency on Moscow both reflects and strains, particularly after the 2022 Kazakhstan crisis.

Andrei Guryev & family.name: PhosAgro Board of Directors
Andrei Guryev & family.description: The governing body of Russia's largest phosphate fertilizer producer, a globally systemically important supplier of crop nutrients whose export flows give the Guryev family and the Kremlin leverage over global food security.

Andrei Guryev & family.name: Russian Union of Industrialists and Entrepreneurs (RSPP)
Andrei Guryev & family.description: Russia's peak big-business lobbying body, which serves as the primary institutionalized interface between the Kremlin and oligarchic capital, coordinating positions on sanctions, asset management, and state contracts.

Bassirou Diomaye Faye.name: Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) Authority of Heads of State
Bassirou Diomaye Faye.description: The supreme political body of West Africa's regional bloc, where Faye is repositioning Senegal as a voice for sovereignty and reform amid the bloc's crisis of legitimacy following Sahel coups.

Bassirou Diomaye Faye.name: African Union Assembly of Heads of State
Bassirou Diomaye Faye.description: The AU's supreme organ, through which Faye is projecting Senegal's pan-Africanist and non-aligned posture as the country renegotiates resource contracts and military basing agreements with Western powers.

Kais Saied.name: Arab League Council
Kais Saied.description: The principal pan-Arab intergovernmental forum, where Saied uses Tunisia's Mediterranean position and his nationalist-populist stance to navigate between Gulf donors, the EU migration agenda, and African solidarity politics.

Kais Saied.name: African Union Assembly of Heads of State
Kais Saied.description: The AU's supreme organ, through which Saied has sought African legitimacy and Chinese and Gulf investment partnerships as he distances Tunisia from IMF conditionality and Western democratic oversight.

Alessandra Galloni.name: Reuters Editorial Board
Alessandra Galloni.description: The governing editorial body of Reuters News Agency, shaping global news framing and information flows across financial, political, and conflict reporting worldwide.

Alessandra Galloni.name: Thomson Reuters Executive Leadership
Alessandra Galloni.description: The senior management cluster of Thomson Reuters Corporation, which sets strategic direction for one of the world's most influential wire services and financial data providers.

Alessandra Galloni.name: World Economic Forum Media Leaders Network
Alessandra Galloni.description: A WEF-affiliated grouping of top global media editors and publishers that shapes elite narrative coordination on issues including climate, geopolitics, and technology governance.

Mark Rutte.name: NATO North Atlantic Council
Mark Rutte.description: The principal political decision-making body of NATO, where Rutte as Secretary General chairs consensus-building among 32 member states on collective defense and strategic posture toward Russia and China.

Mark Rutte.name: NATO-Ukraine Commission
Mark Rutte.description: The formal bilateral institutional body coordinating NATO's political and military support to Ukraine, central to managing Western alliance cohesion in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war.

Mark Rutte.name: G7 Leaders Coordination Group
Mark Rutte.description: The informal but structurally significant network of G7 heads of government and key institutional leaders that aligns Western policy on sanctions, defense spending, and China strategy.

Micky Arison.name: Carnival Corporation Board of Directors
Micky Arison.description: The governing board of the world's largest cruise conglomerate, controlling significant global maritime logistics, port relationships, and sovereign tourism dependencies across dozens of nations.

Micky Arison.name: Miami Heat Ownership Group
Micky Arison.description: The ownership and investment cluster around the NBA's Miami Heat, embedded in Florida's political-economic elite and broader US entertainment and real estate capital networks.

Micky Arison.name: Young Presidents' Organization (YPO) — Florida Chapter
Micky Arison.description: An elite peer network of CEOs and major business owners that functions as a soft-power coordination layer among high-net-worth individuals influencing regional and national policy.

Alexander Karp.name: Palantir Technologies Executive Leadership
Alexander Karp.description: The C-suite and board of Palantir, which supplies AI-driven data analytics and surveillance infrastructure to US, UK, and allied intelligence agencies and militaries, making it a core node in the Western defense-tech complex.

Alexander Karp.name: US Defense Innovation Board — Industry Partners
Alexander Karp.description: An informal but operationally significant cluster of defense-tech CEOs engaged with the Pentagon's modernization agenda, shaping AI and data strategy for US military dominance.

Alexander Karp.name: NATO Innovation Fund Advisory Circle
Alexander Karp.description: A grouping of deep-tech investors and executives aligned with NATO's €1 billion dual-use technology fund, positioning allied defense-tech ecosystems against Chinese and Russian capabilities.

Masayoshi Son.name: SoftBank Group Executive Committee
Masayoshi Son.description: The inner leadership of SoftBank, which deploys hundreds of billions in venture capital globally, shaping AI, semiconductor, and telecom ecosystems across the US, Asia, and the Middle East.

Masayoshi Son.name: Vision Fund Investment Committee
Masayoshi Son.description: The decision-making body of SoftBank's Vision Fund series, the world's largest technology investment vehicle, with structural influence over which AI and tech firms achieve global scale.

Masayoshi Son.name: Saudi Arabia-Japan Investment Forum
Masayoshi Son.description: A bilateral investment coordination body linking SoftBank's capital with Saudi sovereign wealth (PIF), representing a key node in petrodollar recycling into global technology infrastructure.

Yoweri Museveni.name: African Union Assembly
Yoweri Museveni.description: The heads-of-state body of the 55-member African Union, where Museveni as a long-serving leader wields disproportionate influence over East African security architecture and continental geopolitical alignment debates.

Yoweri Museveni.name: East African Community Summit
Yoweri Museveni.description: The apex decision-making body of the EAC regional bloc, critical to managing the DRC conflict, regional trade integration, and the strategic competition between Chinese BRI investment and Western development finance in East Africa.

Yoweri Museveni.name: Non-Aligned Movement Core Group
Yoweri Museveni.description: The informal caucus of states maintaining strategic ambiguity between US and Chinese blocs, within which Museveni has historically positioned Uganda to extract maximum bilateral leverage from competing great powers.

Paul Tudor Jones, II..name: Tudor Investment Corp Leadership
Paul Tudor Jones, II..description: The executive core of PTJ's global macro hedge fund, which moves significant capital across sovereign bond, currency, and commodity markets, with systemic influence on emerging market financial stability.

Paul Tudor Jones, II..name: JUST Capital Board
Paul Tudor Jones, II..description: A US nonprofit and corporate ranking body co-founded by PTJ that pressures Fortune 500 companies on stakeholder capitalism metrics, functioning as a soft-power lever on US corporate governance norms.

Paul Tudor Jones, II..name: Robin Hood Foundation Board
Paul Tudor Jones, II..description: A major New York-based philanthropic organization with deep ties to Wall Street and Democratic Party donor networks, shaping urban policy priorities and elite social capital formation in US financial centers.

Cai Qi.name: CPC Central Committee Secretariat
Cai Qi.description: The administrative nerve center of the Chinese Communist Party, which Cai Qi heads as First Secretary, controlling information flow, document management, and agenda-setting directly for Xi Jinping and the Politburo Standing Committee.

Cai Qi.name: CPC Politburo Standing Committee Support Structure
Cai Qi.description: The institutional apparatus directly serving China's seven-member apex leadership body, giving Cai Qi gatekeeper authority over the most sensitive decisions in Chinese governance and grand strategy.

Cai Qi.name: Beijing Municipal CPC Committee (former)
Cai Qi.description: Cai Qi's former power base as Beijing Party Secretary, which built his network across the capital's security, media, and administrative apparatus and remains a key component of his political alliance structure.

Amancio Ortega.name: Inditex Board of Directors
Amancio Ortega.description: The governing board of the world's largest fast-fashion conglomerate, controlling a global supply chain spanning Asia, Africa, and Europe that is deeply embedded in trade policy, labor regulation, and logistics geopolitics.

Amancio Ortega.name: Pontegadea Inversiones
Amancio Ortega.description: Ortega's private real estate and investment holding company, one of Europe's largest property portfolios, with strategic holdings in prime commercial real estate across the US, UK, and EU that constitute quiet but substantial financial-geopolitical leverage.

Amancio Ortega.name: Spanish Business Council (CEOE)
Amancio Ortega.description: Spain's principal employers' federation and peak business lobbying body, through which Ortega's interests intersect with Spanish government trade, labor, and EU policy formation.

Volodymyr Zelenskyy.name: Ukraine National Security and Defense Council
Volodymyr Zelenskyy.description: Ukraine's apex civil-military coordination body chaired by Zelenskyy, directing wartime strategy, resource allocation, and diplomatic positioning in the ongoing conflict with Russia.

Volodymyr Zelenskyy.name: NATO-Ukraine Commission
Volodymyr Zelenskyy.description: The formal bilateral body linking Ukraine to NATO's political and military decision-making, through which Zelenskyy coordinates weapons supply, intelligence sharing, and the long-term path toward alliance membership.

Volodymyr Zelenskyy.name: Crimea Platform
Volodymyr Zelenskyy.description: A Ukrainian-initiated multilateral diplomatic forum grouping dozens of states and international organizations around the goal of de-occupying Crimea, functioning as Zelenskyy's primary instrument for sustaining international normative and political pressure on Russia.

Ken Griffin.name: Citadel Investment Group Executive Committee
Ken Griffin.description: The leadership core of one of the world's most influential hedge funds and market-making operations, shaping global liquidity and US financial market structure.

Ken Griffin.name: Republican Mega-Donor Network
Ken Griffin.description: A coalition of ultra-high-net-worth conservative donors coordinating political spending to influence US elections, regulatory policy, and Supreme Court appointments.

Ken Griffin.name: Chicago Council on Global Affairs
Ken Griffin.description: A premier US foreign policy think tank where financial elites and policymakers align on trade, security, and international economic strategy.

Liu Yongxing.name: CCP United Front Work Department Affiliated Business Network
Liu Yongxing.description: A web of CCP-linked private sector leaders whose business activities and overseas operations serve broader Chinese state strategic and influence objectives.

Liu Yongxing.name: East Hope Group Board
Liu Yongxing.description: The controlling entity of one of China's largest private conglomerates spanning aluminum, chemicals, and agriculture, with supply chain leverage across Asia and Africa.

Liu Yongxing.name: Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC)
Liu Yongxing.description: China's top political advisory body through which major private sector figures maintain formal ties to CCP leadership and signal policy alignment.

Stanley Druckenmiller.name: Duquesne Family Office Executive Circle
Stanley Druckenmiller.description: The private investment vehicle of one of the most respected macro traders globally, whose market calls and public statements move sovereign bond and currency markets.

Stanley Druckenmiller.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York Advisory Circles
Stanley Druckenmiller.description: An informal but influential network of top macro investors and Wall Street figures who engage with the NY Fed on monetary policy transmission and financial stability.

Stanley Druckenmiller.name: Paul Tudor Jones–Stanley Druckenmiller Macro Investor Network
Stanley Druckenmiller.description: An informal coalition of elite global macro hedge fund managers who coordinate views on fiscal policy, inflation, and geopolitical risk with systemic market influence.

Tô Lâm.name: CPV Politburo (Communist Party of Vietnam)
Tô Lâm.description: Vietnam's supreme decision-making body that sets national strategy on security, foreign policy, and economic development amid US-China competition.

Tô Lâm.name: ASEAN Heads of State Summit
Tô Lâm.description: The apex forum of Southeast Asian leaders where Vietnam plays a pivotal balancing role between US alliance structures and Chinese economic integration.

Tô Lâm.name: Vietnam Ministry of Public Security Leadership Council
Tô Lâm.description: The institutional base from which Tô Lâm built his power, overseeing internal security, surveillance infrastructure, and counter-intelligence with significant geopolitical implications.

Demis Hassabis.name: Google DeepMind Executive Leadership
Demis Hassabis.description: The operational core of Alphabet's flagship AI research and deployment arm, driving breakthroughs in frontier AI that reshape scientific, military, and economic competition.

Demis Hassabis.name: UK AI Safety Institute Advisory Network
Demis Hassabis.description: A British government-convened body of leading AI researchers and executives shaping national and allied AI governance frameworks in competition with Chinese and US regulatory models.

Demis Hassabis.name: Frontier Model Forum
Demis Hassabis.description: An industry coalition of leading AI labs including Google DeepMind, OpenAI, Microsoft, and Anthropic coordinating on AI safety standards and influencing global regulatory norms.

Mohammed VI of Morocco.name: Arab League Summit
Mohammed VI of Morocco.description: The principal multilateral forum of Arab states where Morocco exercises diplomatic influence on regional security, migration, and normalization dynamics.

Mohammed VI of Morocco.name: African Union Assembly
Mohammed VI of Morocco.description: The continental heads-of-state body where Morocco, since its 2017 readmission, projects influence over African infrastructure, security, and trade architecture.

Mohammed VI of Morocco.name: Morocco-US Strategic Partnership Council

Pavel Durov.name: Telegram Messenger (Executive Leadership)

Gautam Adani.name: Adani Group Executive Council

Gautam Adani.description: India's premier industry body whose senior membership shapes trade policy, foreign investment frameworks, and government-business relations at the national and G20 level.

Martin Lau.name: Tencent Board of Directors

Martin Lau.description: Internal body overseeing Tencent's vast global venture and strategic investment portfolio, making it a key node in shaping the global tech startup ecosystem and AI development landscape.

Charles Schwab.name: Charles Schwab Corporation Board of Directors

Susanne Klatten.name: BMW AG Supervisory Board

Faisal Bin Qassim Al Thani.name: Al Faisal Holding Executive Committee
Faisal Bin Qassim Al Thani.description: Leadership body of one of Qatar's largest diversified private conglomerates, with investments spanning hospitality, real estate, and finance that amplify Qatari soft power regionally and globally.

Faisal Bin Qassim Al Thani.name: Qatar Business Association (QBA) Leadership
Faisal Bin Qassim Al Thani.description: Institutional body representing Qatar's major private-sector interests, bridging the royal-linked business elite with state economic planning and foreign investment strategy.

Faisal Bin Qassim Al Thani.name: Doha Forum Advisory Network
Faisal Bin Qassim Al Thani.description: High-level geopolitical dialogue platform hosted in Qatar where Gulf business and political elites engage with global leaders, reinforcing Qatar's role as a neutral diplomatic and financial hub.

Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.name: Egyptian Supreme Council of the Armed Forces (SCAF)
Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.description: Egypt's apex military governance body, which controls vast segments of the national economy and remains the foundational source of political authority underpinning el-Sisi's rule.

Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.name: Arab League Summit Leadership
Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.description: The primary multilateral forum of Arab states where el-Sisi projects Egyptian leadership ambitions, mediates regional conflicts, and aligns Cairo's posture on Palestine, Sudan, and Libya.

Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.name: African Union (AU) Peace and Security Council
Abdel Fattah el-Sisi.description: Continental security body in which Egypt plays an active role, particularly regarding Nile Basin disputes, Sahel instability, and counterterrorism frameworks affecting North and East Africa.

Ricardo Salinas Pliego & family.name: Grupo Salinas Executive Committee
Ricardo Salinas Pliego & family.description: Central leadership body of Mexico's second-largest business conglomerate spanning retail, banking, telecoms, and media, giving the family structural leverage over Mexican consumer finance and information ecosystems.

Ricardo Salinas Pliego & family.name: Banco Azteca Board of Directors
Ricardo Salinas Pliego & family.description: Governing body of one of Latin America's largest retail banks by customer base, providing the Salinas family with deep reach into underbanked populations and informal economy financial flows across Mexico and Central America.

Ricardo Salinas Pliego & family.name: Mexican Business Coordinating Council (CCE)
Ricardo Salinas Pliego & family.description: Mexico's apex private-sector coordination body that engages directly with the federal government on trade, investment, and regulatory policy, including USMCA implementation and energy sector disputes.

Cyril Ramaphosa.name: African National Congress (ANC) National Executive Committee
Cyril Ramaphosa.description: The ruling party's supreme decision-making body between conferences, which sets South Africa's domestic and foreign policy direction and remains the primary arena of Ramaphosa's political power.

Cyril Ramaphosa.name: African Union (AU) Assembly of Heads of State
Cyril Ramaphosa.description: The continent's highest political body where Ramaphosa has served as AU Champion on COVID-19 and peace initiatives, projecting South Africa as a continental diplomatic anchor.

Cyril Ramaphosa.description: The premier multilateral forum of major emerging economies, where South Africa under Ramaphosa has championed BRICS expansion and positioned Pretoria as a bridge between the Global South and both Western and Chinese blocs.

Phil Knight & family.name: Nike Inc. Board of Directors
Phil Knight & family.description: Governs the world's dominant sportswear corporation, wielding significant influence over global supply chains, labor standards, and consumer culture across Asia and the Americas.

Phil Knight & family.name: Swoosh LLC (Knight Family Office)
Phil Knight & family.description: Private family investment vehicle controlling majority Nike stake and philanthropic capital, operating as a concentrated node of billionaire soft power in media, education, and venture funding.

Phil Knight & family.name: University of Oregon Athletic & Academic Donor Network
Phil Knight & family.description: Elite donor coalition shaping U.S. university athletics and research funding, functioning as a soft-power cultural influence cluster tied to American brand diplomacy.

Taye Atske Selassie.name: African Union Assembly of Heads of State
Taye Atske Selassie.description: The AU's supreme decision-making body, where Ethiopia—as host nation and founding power—exercises outsized agenda-setting influence over pan-African governance and security frameworks.

Taye Atske Selassie.name: Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD)
Taye Atske Selassie.description: East African regional bloc addressing conflict, migration, and development, where Ethiopia's size and location make it a pivotal actor in Horn of Africa stability negotiations.

Taye Atske Selassie.name: Nile Basin Initiative

Lai Ching-te.name: Taiwan Presidential Office & National Security Council
Lai Ching-te.description: The apex executive and security coordination body of the Republic of China government, directing Taiwan's defense posture, cross-strait policy, and alliance management with the United States and Japan.

Lai Ching-te.name: Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) Central Executive Committee
Lai Ching-te.description: Governing party leadership council that sets Taiwan's sovereignty-affirming political agenda, directly shaping the ideological framing of US-China-Taiwan triangular competition.

Lai Ching-te.name: US-Taiwan Defense Industry Forum (Track 1.5 Network)
Lai Ching-te.description: Semi-official institutional channel linking Taiwan's government with U.S. defense contractors and congressional allies, facilitating arms procurement and deterrence signaling against PLA pressure.

Rafaela Aponte-Diamant.name: MSC (Mediterranean Shipping Company) Executive Leadership
Rafaela Aponte-Diamant.description: Directs the world's largest container shipping line by fleet capacity, controlling a critical chokepoint in global trade logistics that intersects with port infrastructure, sanctions enforcement, and supply chain geopolitics.

Rafaela Aponte-Diamant.name: World Shipping Council
Rafaela Aponte-Diamant.description: The principal industry association representing major ocean carriers before international regulators and governments, where MSC's dominance gives Aponte-Diamant structural influence over global maritime rule-setting.

Rafaela Aponte-Diamant.name: MSC Foundation & Philanthropy Network
Rafaela Aponte-Diamant.description: Family-controlled philanthropic arm channeling resources into Mediterranean and African development projects, extending the Aponte-Diamant family's soft-power footprint beyond commercial logistics.

Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb.name: Al-Azhar Supreme Council (Majma' al-Buhuth al-Islamiyya)
Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb.description: The highest scholarly authority of Al-Azhar, the world's most influential Sunni Islamic institution, issuing fatwas and theological guidance that shape religious discourse for approximately 1.5 billion Muslims globally.

Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb.name: Muslim Council of Elders (Majlis Hukama' al-Muslimin)
Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb.description: Abu Dhabi-based international body of senior Islamic scholars co-chaired by el-Tayeb, designed to counter extremist narratives and project a state-aligned moderate Islam as a soft-power instrument across conflict zones.

Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb.name: World Religious Leaders Summit Network (Abu Dhabi Declaration Signatories)
Grand Imam Ahmed el-Tayeb.description: Interfaith coalition formalized through the 2019 Human Fraternity document signed with Pope Francis, positioning el-Tayeb as a key broker in Vatican-Islamic world diplomacy and Gulf-backed religious multilateralism.

Rupert Murdoch & family.name: News Corp Board of Directors
Rupert Murdoch & family.description: Governs the global print and digital media empire spanning the U.S., UK, and Australia, functioning as a structural node for conservative political agenda-setting and information-environment shaping across anglophone democracies.

Rupert Murdoch & family.name: Fox Corporation Board of Directors
Rupert Murdoch & family.description: Controls the most-watched U.S. cable news network, making it a decisive force in American electoral politics, Republican Party alignment, and the domestic framing of US-China and NATO policy debates.

Rupert Murdoch & family.name: Murdoch Family Trust
Rupert Murdoch & family.description: The controlling shareholder vehicle through which the Murdoch family exercises ultimate governance over both News Corp and Fox Corporation, concentrating unprecedented private media power across three continents.

Hamad II of Bahrain.name: Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Supreme Council
Hamad II of Bahrain.description: The apex body of the six-member Gulf Arab bloc coordinating defense, economic, and foreign policy, where Bahrain's role as host of the U.S. Fifth Fleet amplifies its strategic weight beyond its small size.

Hamad II of Bahrain.name: Bahrain Supreme Defence Council
Hamad II of Bahrain.description: The king's direct command authority over Bahraini armed forces and the framework governing the U.S.-Bahrain Defense Cooperation Agreement, making it a key node in American military basing strategy in the Persian Gulf.

Hamad II of Bahrain.name: Abraham Accords Normalization Network
Hamad II of Bahrain.description: The informal coalition of Arab states that normalized relations with Israel from 2020 onward, in which Bahrain participates as a founding member, reshaping Gulf-Israel security cooperation and containing Iranian influence.

Mathias Cormann.name: OECD Secretary-General's Office
Mathias Cormann.description: Leads the 38-member Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development, the world's primary standard-setting body for tax policy, trade rules, digital regulation, and economic governance among advanced economies.

Mathias Cormann.name: Global Forum on Transparency and Exchange of Information for Tax Purposes
Mathias Cormann.description: OECD-hosted multilateral body with over 160 member jurisdictions enforcing international tax transparency standards, where Cormann's leadership directly shapes the rules constraining offshore financial flows and corporate tax avoidance.

Mathias Cormann.name: G20 Finance Track (OECD Sherpa Network)

Naruhito.name: Imperial Household of Japan (Kunaichō)

Naruhito.name: UN Water Advisory Board (Honorary Patron Network)

Subrahmanyam Jaishankar.name: India's Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS)

Subrahmanyam Jaishankar.description: The ministerial-level coordination forum of the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (US, Japan, Australia, India), where Jaishankar is India's principal voice in the Indo-Pacific's most consequential security alignment against Chinese maritime expansion.

Subrahmanyam Jaishankar.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Council of Foreign Ministers

Joseph Tsai.name: Alibaba Group Board of Directors

Joseph Tsai.name: Brooklyn Nets / BSE Global Ownership Group

Frederik X of Denmark.name: Danish Royal House (Kongehuset)

Howard Marks.name: Oaktree Capital Management Investment Committee

Howard Marks.name: Brookings Institution Board of Trustees

Howard Marks.name: Council on Foreign Relations Corporate Member Network

Terry Gou.name: Foxconn Technology Group (Hon Hai Precision) Board

Robin Zeng.name: CATL Executive Board

Esmail Qaani.name: IRGC Quds Force Command

Lim Kok Thay.name: Genting Group Board of Directors

Lim Kok Thay.name: Malaysia-China Business Council

Claudia Sheinbaum.name: Mexican Cabinet (Gabinete de México)

Shen Haixiong.description: State broadcaster overseeing CCTV, CGTN, CNR, and China Radio International, which Shen leads as the CCP's primary instrument for domestic narrative control and global information-influence operations.

Shen Haixiong.description: Party organ that directs ideological messaging across all state media, with CMG as its flagship broadcaster, integrating Shen's operations into the CCP's global discourse-shaping strategy.

Shen Haixiong.name: CGTN International Broadcasting Alliance

Robin Li.name: Baidu Executive Board

Robin Li.name: China AI Industry Alliance (AIIA)

Robin Li.name: Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC)
Robin Li.description: CCP's top advisory political body in which Li holds membership, embedding him in elite Party-state consultation networks that align major tech entrepreneurs with national strategic priorities.

Zhang Yiming.name: ByteDance Board of Directors
Zhang Yiming.description: Governing body of the world's most valuable private tech company, whose TikTok platform shapes global information flows and sits at the center of US-China data sovereignty disputes.

Zhang Yiming.name: China Internet Investment Fund (CIIF) Portfolio Network
Zhang Yiming.description: State-backed investment vehicle with stakes in major Chinese internet platforms, structurally linking ByteDance to CCP regulatory and strategic oversight.

Zhang Yiming.name: China Entrepreneurs Forum
Zhang Yiming.description: Elite network of China's top private-sector billionaires that coordinates informal influence between the tech industry and Beijing's economic policymakers.

William Alexander of the Netherlands.name: Dutch Royal House (Koninklijk Huis)

William Alexander of the Netherlands.name: NATO Heads of State Council

William Alexander of the Netherlands.name: EU Council of Heads of State and Government (European Council)

Jayshree Ullal.name: Arista Networks Board of Directors
Jayshree Ullal.description: Governing body of a dominant cloud networking infrastructure company whose technology underpins hyperscaler data centers critical to US AI and military cloud competition with China.

Jayshree Ullal.name: Stanford University Engineering Advisory Council
Jayshree Ullal.description: Elite advisory network connecting Silicon Valley's top technologists to the US's leading STEM research pipeline, shaping talent flows into strategic tech sectors.

Jayshree Ullal.name: US Semiconductor and Cloud Infrastructure CEO Roundtable (informal)
Jayshree Ullal.description: Informal coalition of US cloud and networking hardware executives that engages with Commerce Department and USTR on export controls and supply chain policy.

Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.name: Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Supreme Council
Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.description: The apex intergovernmental body of Gulf monarchies that coordinates regional security, energy, and economic policy amid US-Iran tensions and great-power competition.

Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.name: Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) Supreme Council
Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.description: Sovereign wealth fund governing body controlling over $450 billion in global assets, giving Qatar structural leverage in Western financial markets, media, and infrastructure.

Vladimir Lisin.name: Russian Union of Industrialists and Entrepreneurs (RSPP)
